how_to_apply: 1. REPLACE: Find the template with matching 'id' in question_templates.json and replace the entire object with the one provided in 'replace[]'.
2. ADD_AFTER: Find the template with 'after_id' and insert the new template object immediately after it in the 'templates' array.
3. ADD_TO_CURATED_BANKS: Add the new bank key and metadata to the 'curated_banks' object.
4. After applying, verify: count should be 60 + 8 = 68 templates total.
5. Also apply the corresponding updates in verification_additions.py.

summary_of_changes.T-7M-04: REPLACED — content changed from supplementary/complementary angles (wrong level) to actual VC2M7M04 transversal angle relationships
summary_of_changes.T-7M-04b: NEW — supplementary, complementary, vertically opposite angles (valid Y7 content, kept as separate template)
summary_of_changes.T-7M-05: NEW — interior angle sum of triangle; find unknown angle (VC2M7M05)
summary_of_changes.T-8N-01b: NEW — irrational number recognition in context (VC2M8N01, curated_bank)
summary_of_changes.T-8P-01: REPLACED — content changed from experimental probability (wrong code) to complementary events P(A)+P(A')=1 (actual VC2M8P01)
summary_of_changes.T-9A-04c: NEW — distance between two points (part of VC2M9A04)

replacement.topic: Angle relationships when parallel lines are cut by a transversal
replacement.question_template: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One angle is {a}°. The marked angle is {relationship} to this angle. What is the size of the marked angle?
context_variants: Parallel lines are cut by a transversal. An angle of {a}° is formed. What is the size of the {relationship} angle?
Lines AB and CD are parallel. A transversal crosses both. One angle measures {a}°. What is the {relationship} angle?
replacement.generation_constraint: a ∈ [35..85] for foundation/standard; any value for advanced. Result always positive integer.

replacement.question_template: The probability of event A occurring is {p}. What is the probability of event A NOT occurring?
context_variants: The probability that it rains tomorrow is {p}. What is the probability that it does NOT rain tomorrow?
A student has a {p} probability of winning a game. What is the probability of NOT winning?
The probability of drawing a red card from a deck is {p}. What is the probability of NOT drawing a red card?
replacement.distractor_notes: wrong answers: p itself (forgot to subtract), 1+p (added instead), p/2 (halved), 1-(p+1/denom) (off by one on numerator)

template._note: Supplementary/complementary/vertically opposite — originally misassigned to T-7M-04. Now correctly associated with VC2M7M05 elaboration content. Kept because it is high-frequency drill content.
template.topic: Supplementary, complementary, and vertically opposite angles
template.question_template: Two angles are {relationship}. One angle is {a}°. What is the other angle?
context_variants: Angle ABC = {a}°. Angle ABC and angle DEF are {relationship}. What is angle DEF?
An angle measures {a}°. What is its {relationship_noun}?

template.topic: Interior angle sum of triangle; find unknown angle
template.question_template: A triangle has angles of {a}° and {b}°. What is the size of the third angle?
context_variants: In triangle ABC, angle A = {a}° and angle B = {b}°. What is angle C?
Two angles of a triangle measure {a}° and {b}°. Find the third angle.
A triangle has one angle of {a}° and another of {b}°. What is the remaining angle?

b.rule: b chosen so a + b < 180 and third angle c = 180 - a - b ∈ [15, 145]
b.foundation_range: a + b ∈ [60, 120] so c is always reasonable
b.advanced_range: a + b ∈ [30, 165], includes very obtuse triangles

template.distractor_notes: distractors: 180-(a+b)+5, 360-(a+b), a+b (student adds instead of subtracts from 180)
template.generation_constraint: a + b < 180; third angle = 180 - a - b must be ≥ 15 and ≤ 150; all three angles must sum to exactly 180

template.topic: Recognise irrational numbers including π and square roots of non-perfect-squares
template.question_template: Which of the following is an irrational number?
template.bank_note: 3 options are rational (including traps: √4, 22/7, 0.333..., terminating decimals), 1 is irrational (√2, √3, √5, π, √50 etc). Exploit common misconceptions at Year 8 level.

template.topic: Midpoint of a line segment between two points on the Cartesian plane
template.question_template: What is the midpoint of the line segment joining ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2})?
context_variants: Find the midpoint of the interval from A({x1},{y1}) to B({x2},{y2}).
Points P({x1},{y1}) and Q({x2},{y2}) are the endpoints of a line segment. What are the coordinates of the midpoint?

x2.rule: x2 = x1 + 2k where k ∈ [-4, 4]\{0}, ensuring midpoint x = x1+k is integer

y2.rule: y2 = y1 + 2j where j ∈ [-4, 4]\{0}, ensuring midpoint y = y1+j is integer

template.distractor_notes: distractors: (x2-x1, y2-y1) distance not midpoint; (x1+x2, y1+y2) forgot to divide by 2; midpoint shifted by ±1 on one coordinate
template.generation_constraint: midpoint coordinates must both be integers — use only even differences between x1,x2 and y1,y2

template.topic: Distance between two points on the Cartesian plane
template.question_template: What is the distance between the points ({x1},{y1}) and ({x2},{y2})?
context_variants: Find the length of the line segment from A({x1},{y1}) to B({x2},{y2}).
Calculate the distance between P({x1},{y1}) and Q({x2},{y2}). Give your answer as an exact value or to 2 decimal places.

pythagorean_triple.note: use Pythagorean triples to guarantee integer distances; scale by 1 or 2

template.distractor_notes: distractors: Δx + Δy (Manhattan distance), (Δx)²+(Δy)² (forgot square root), (Δx)+(Δy) just adding, distance±1
template.generation_constraint: always use Pythagorean triples scaled so distance is a clean integer; avoids surd answers that make MCQ ambiguous

template.topic: Calculate percentage error between measured and actual values
template.question_template: A measurement is recorded as {measured}. The actual value is {actual}. What is the percentage error?
context_variants: A student measures a length as {measured} cm. The exact length is {actual} cm. Calculate the percentage error.
An estimate of {measured} is made for a quantity whose true value is {actual}. What is the percentage error (to 1 decimal place)?

measured.rule: measured = actual × (1 + error_pct/100) if over, or actual × (1 - error_pct/100) if under

template.distractor_notes: distractors: absolute error not percentage, (measured-actual)/measured × 100 (wrong denominator), error×2, error/2
template.generation_constraint: measured must be a clean integer (use only error_pct values that make actual × pct/100 an integer); answer is always a clean percentage like 5.0% or 10.0%

irrational_recognition_bank.covers: Year 8 recognition of irrational numbers; traps include √4, 22/7, 0.333..., 3.14; correct answers include √2, √3, √5, √7, π, √50
